prompt:
Board-games club again — this time it's the Othello ladder that needs a referee. Same non-negotiable as always: two laptops replaying the same game must produce byte-identical logs, so every ordering below is a contract, not a suggestion. Implement src/lib.rs in this crate (rust-reversi); tests/reversi.rs and tests/fixtures/ are reviewed and read-only, as are Cargo.toml and .gitignore. Stdlib only.

Board: 8x8, squares named file a-h + rank 1-8 ("d3"); rank 1 is the FIRST line of every render/fixture. Cells 'B', 'W', '.'.

pub struct Game:
- Game::new() — standard start (d4 W, e4 B, d5 B, e5 W... i.e. the usual centre: row 4 shows ...WB..., row 5 shows ...BW...), Black to move. Also impl Default.
- Game::load(fixture: &str) -> Result<Game, String> — first non-blank line exactly 'turn: B' or 'turn: W' (else "bad turn line: <line>", or "missing turn line" on empty input), then 8 rows of 8 cells; blank lines skipped, trailing spaces tolerated. Errors: "expected 8 board rows, got 7", "row 3 must be 8 cells, got 7", "bad cell at d4: 'x'" (square name, first offender scanning row-major).
- render() — the 8 rows joined with \n, no turn line, no trailing newline. turn() -> char, counts() -> (u32, u32) as (black, white).
- Reversi legality: a move goes on an empty square and must flank at least one line of opponent discs (any of the 8 directions, contiguous opponents terminated by your own disc); ALL flanked lines flip. legal_moves() -> Vec<String> in row-major order (rank 1 outermost, file a first). apply(mv: &str) -> Result<(), String>: "bad move: <mv>" for anything not a well-formed square or 'pass', "illegal move: <mv>" if it doesn't flip, and the forced-pass rule: apply("pass") succeeds ONLY when the mover has no legal move (else "cannot pass: legal moves exist") and just flips the turn. Once neither side can move the game is over: is_over(), and every apply returns "game over". Failed applies change nothing.
- winner() -> Option<char> — None while running, then 'B', 'W', or '=' on equal counts.
- bot_move() -> Option<String> — greedy: the legal move that flips the MOST discs, ties broken by row-major order (first such square wins); Some("pass") when stuck but not over; None when over. Never mutates.

pub fn transcript(opening: &str) -> Result<String, String> — start a fresh game, replay the whitespace-separated opening moves (an apply error aborts as "opening move 3: illegal move: c3" — 1-based numbering, the apply message verbatim after the colon), then the greedy bot plays BOTH sides to the end. Output, \n-joined with no trailing newline: one line per move (opening and bot alike, one running counter) 'move 12 (W): e2', then 'final:', the 8 render lines, 'black: 19 white: 45', 'winner: W' (or '=').

The suite pins the opening move list and bot choice, an 8-direction flip on a radial fixture, forced-pass and finished-game behavior on an endgame fixture, a drawn full board, exact counts, and two COMPLETE 60-move transcripts (bot-vs-bot from the start, and from the 6-move fixture opening in tests/fixtures/opening.txt) compared string-equal. Acceptance: cargo test --offline green.
